heuristics.trigger: ASan stack-use-after-return in bigint/rational float conversion; crash frame reads 'pool_storage' from a returned mrb_bint_reduce frame — a persistent bigint holding a pointer into transient/stack pool memory.
heuristics.root_cause_lesson: bint_new took ownership of an mpz via mpz_move even when x->p aliases the arena/stack pool. The pool unwinds on return, so the heap bigint's limb pointer dangles and later reads (as_float) hit freed stack.
heuristics.how_to_apply: In bint_new heap branch, detect is_pool_memory(x, MPZ_POOL(ctx)); if so, deep-copy limbs into fresh heap (mpz_set into zeroed as.heap, then mpz_clear x) instead of mpz_move. Only move-pointer when x owns heap memory.
